issues: The draft describes only the 'Eagles nip Sockeyes' article, but the OCR page also contains a second article about the Nanaimo Clippers' 9-7 win over Delta Flyers, which directly mentions the Richmond Sockeyes as Nanaimo's next opponent. The long description should acknowledge this secondary Sockeyes mention.
Mike Clairinbull is a misspelling carried over from the OCR — the correct name is Mike Claringbull.

Jamie Stewart is listed in the draft as Langley's goaltender, but Jamie Stewart is actually a Richmond Sockeyes player. The OCR says 'Jamie Stewart stopped 46 shots for Langley' — this is likely an OCR/source error or a different Jamie Stewart; the draft should not present this as fact without a flag, but the name as printed in the OCR is 'Jamie Stewart' for Langley.

The byline 'The Canadian Press' applies only to the first article. The second article on the page (Nanaimo Clippers vs Delta) has no byline in the OCR and is from a different source. The draft does not reflect this second article at all.
The long description omits the secondary Sockeyes-relevant detail: the Nanaimo Clippers article mentions Richmond Sockeyes as Nanaimo's next opponent that night (Jan 3, 8 o'clock, Civic Arena).
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Langley Eagles defeated the Richmond Sockeyes 5-4 in BCJHL action Friday night, with Neil Eisenhut scoring twice and Tony Bobbitt netting two for Richmond; a separate article notes the Nanaimo Clippers were set to face the first-place Sockeyes later that same night.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: Neil Eisenhut scored twice to lead the Langley Eagles to a 5-4 win over the Richmond Sockeyes in B.C. Junior Hockey League play Friday night. Tony Bobbitt scored twice for Richmond, with singles from Bill Hardy and Mike Claringbull; Jamie Stewart stopped 46 shots for Langley while Richmond netminder Glen Pencer made 27 saves. A second article on the same page covers the Nanaimo Clippers' 9-7 win over the Delta Flyers, and notes the Clippers were scheduled to face first-place Richmond Sockeyes that same evening at 8 o'clock at Civic Arena.
review.notes: The OCR page contains two articles: (1) the CP wire story "Eagles nip Sockeyes" covering the Langley 5-4 win; and (2) a local Nanaimo Clippers game recap that mentions Richmond Sockeyes as that night's opponent. Both are Sockeyes-relevant and are reflected in the corrected description. Note also that "Jamie Stewart" appears as Langley's goaltender in the OCR — this conflicts with Jamie Stewart being a known Sockeyes player; editor should verify whether this is a different person or an OCR/source error. "Mike Clairinbull" in the OCR is corrected to "Mike Claringbull" in the description.
